rendered_prompt:
You are repairing an existing DALi dali-ui Markdown guide.
Return only the complete repaired Markdown document.
Do not rewrite the guide. Only edit the listed issue spans and the smallest surrounding context needed.
Preserve unrelated headings, ordering, and correct content as-is.
Keep the repaired user-facing guide in en only.
Remove Korean, Japanese, Chinese, or other non-English prose when it appears in the guide.
Do not invent APIs. Use only provided allowed symbols, property owners, usage patterns, or local helpers defined in a code block.

Source-grounded repair requirements:
- Repair only the problematic spans and the smallest surrounding context needed; do not rewrite the whole document.
- For each listed issue, use the repository evidence and context pack to verify the corrected wording or code before returning.
- If an issue reveals the same API misuse nearby, fix the nearby equivalent misuse too; do not make unrelated style rewrites.
- After repair, re-check the edited lines against public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when needed.
- For every edited C++ code block, inline API statement, and backticked API name, verify:
  - receiver method exists on the receiver type
  - static methods are called statically and member methods are called on valid instances
  - assignment target is compatible with the method return type
  - argument count, argument type, and overload shape match the public API
  - enum owner and enum value both exist
  - property constants use the correct owner and value type
  - signal callback signatures match the public signal typedef
  - code blocks do not rely on inferred namespaces or overloads from naming alone

Feature:
signals

Original Markdown:
---
title: Signals
sidebar_label: Signals
category: utilities
---

# Signals

Signals let a dali-ui app react to input, view, resource, lifecycle, and custom events through typed C++ callbacks.

## Table of Contents

- [Connect to dali-ui View Events](#connect-to-dali-ui-view-events)
- [Track Connections with `Dali::ConnectionTracker`](#track-connections-with-daliconnectiontracker)
- [Use Typed `Dali::Signal` Objects](#use-typed-dalisignal-objects)
- [Emit Custom App Signals](#emit-custom-app-signals)
- [Use Return-Valued Signals](#use-return-valued-signals)
- [Connect Functors with `Dali::FunctorDelegate`](#connect-functors-with-dalifunctordelegate)
- [Inspect Signal State](#inspect-signal-state)
- [Signal Behavior During Emission](#signal-behavior-during-emission)

## Connect to dali-ui View Events

In dali-ui application code, connect to signals exposed by app-facing objects such as `Dali::Ui::InteractiveView`, `Dali::Ui::InteractiveTrait` attached to a `Dali::Ui::View`, `Dali::Ui::WebView`, and other dali-ui controls. The signal defines the event stream, and your callback defines the response.

`Dali::Ui::InteractiveTrait::ClickedSignal()` returns a typed `Dali::Signal<void(Dali::Ui::View, Dali::Ui::InputEvent)>`. `Dali::Ui::InteractiveTrait::PressedChangedSignal()` returns a typed `Dali::Signal<void(Dali::Ui::View, bool, Dali::Ui::InputEvent)>`.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/dali-ui-foundation.h>

class ItemController : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  Dali::Ui::View CreateItem()
  {
    Dali::Ui::View item = Dali::Ui::View::New()
      .SetRequestedWidth(320.0f)
      .SetRequestedHeight(72.0f);

    item.AsInteractive([this](Dali::Ui::InteractiveTrait& trait) {
      trait.ClickedSignal().Connect(this, &ItemController::OnItemClicked);

      trait.PressedChangedSignal().Connect(
        this,
        [this](Dali::Ui::View view, bool pressed, Dali::Ui::InputEvent event) {
          OnItemPressedChanged(view, pressed, event);
        });
    });

    return item;
  }

private:
  void OnItemClicked(Dali::Ui::View view, Dali::Ui::InputEvent event)
  {
    mLastClickedView = view;
  }

  void OnItemPressedChanged(Dali::Ui::View view, bool pressed, Dali::Ui::InputEvent event)
  {
    view.SetBackgroundColor(pressed ? Dali::Color::CYAN : Dali::Color::WHITE);
  }

  Dali::Ui::View mLastClickedView;
};
```

For resource or page lifecycle events, connect to the specific signal exposed by the dali-ui object. For example, `Dali::Ui::WebView::PageLoadStartedSignal()` uses `Dali::Signal<void(Dali::Ui::WebView, const Dali::String&)>`, and `Dali::Ui::WebView::FrameRenderedSignal()` uses `Dali::Signal<void(Dali::Ui::WebView)>`.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/dali-ui-foundation.h>

class BrowserController : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  Dali::Ui::WebView CreateWebView()
  {
    Dali::Ui::WebView webView = Dali::Ui::WebView::New();

    webView.PageLoadStartedSignal().Connect(this, &BrowserController::OnPageLoadStarted);
    webView.FrameRenderedSignal().Connect(this, &BrowserController::OnFrameRendered);

    return webView;
  }

private:
  void OnPageLoadStarted(Dali::Ui::WebView webView, const Dali::String& url)
  {
    mCurrentUrl = url;
  }

  void OnFrameRendered(Dali::Ui::WebView webView)
  {
    ++mRenderedFrameCount;
  }

  Dali::String mCurrentUrl;
  unsigned int mRenderedFrameCount{0u};
};
```

## Track Connections with `Dali::ConnectionTracker`

Use `Dali::ConnectionTracker` on controller objects that connect member functions or functors to `Dali::Signal` instances. It implements `Dali::ConnectionTrackerInterface`, so connections made with that tracker are disconnected when the controller is destroyed.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/dali-ui-foundation.h>

class PanelController : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  void Attach(Dali::Ui::View panel)
  {
    panel.AsInteractive([this](Dali::Ui::InteractiveTrait& trait) {
      trait.ClickedSignal().Connect(this, &PanelController::OnPanelClicked);
    });
  }

  void DisableCallbacks()
  {
    DisconnectAll();
  }

  unsigned int ActiveConnectionCount() const
  {
    return GetConnectionCount();
  }

private:
  void OnPanelClicked(Dali::Ui::View view, Dali::Ui::InputEvent event)
  {
    mClicked = true;
  }

  bool mClicked{false};
};
```

`Dali::ConnectionTracker::DisconnectAll()` removes every signal connection associated with the tracker. `Dali::ConnectionTracker::GetConnectionCount()` reports how many connections are currently tracked by that object.

## Use Typed `Dali::Signal` Objects

`Dali::Signal` is a typed signal template. The function signature in the template controls the callback signature and the arguments passed by `Emit()`.

Common forms include `Dali::Signal<void()>`, `Dali::Signal<void(Arg0)>`, `Dali::Signal<void(Arg0, Arg1)>`, `Dali::Signal<void(Arg0, Arg1, Arg2)>`, `Dali::Signal<Ret()>`, `Dali::Signal<Ret(Arg0)>`, `Dali::Signal<Ret(Arg0, Arg1)>`, and `Dali::Signal<Ret(Arg0, Arg1, Arg2)>`.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/dali-ui-foundation.h>

class SelectionModel
{
public:
  using SelectedSignalType = Dali::Signal<void(Dali::Ui::View)>;

  SelectedSignalType& SelectedSignal()
  {
    return mSelectedSignal;
  }

  void Select(Dali::Ui::View view)
  {
    mSelectedView = view;
    mSelectedSignal.Emit(view);
  }

private:
  Dali::Ui::View      mSelectedView;
  SelectedSignalType  mSelectedSignal;
};

class SelectionController : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  void Bind(SelectionModel& model)
  {
    model.SelectedSignal().Connect(this, &SelectionController::OnSelected);
  }

private:
  void OnSelected(Dali::Ui::View selectedView)
  {
    mCurrentSelection = selectedView;
  }

  Dali::Ui::View mCurrentSelection;
};
```

A `Dali::Signal<void(Dali::Ui::View)>` accepts callbacks shaped like `void Callback(Dali::Ui::View)`. A `Dali::Signal<void(Dali::Ui::View, bool, Dali::Ui::InputEvent)>` accepts callbacks shaped like `void Callback(Dali::Ui::View, bool, Dali::Ui::InputEvent)`.

## Emit Custom App Signals

For application-level components, expose a signal accessor and keep the `Dali::Signal` instance as component state. Application code connects through the accessor; the component emits from the point where the event actually happens.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/dali-ui-foundation.h>

class DetailsPane
{
public:
  using OpenedSignalType = Dali::Signal<void(Dali::Ui::View)>;

  OpenedSignalType& OpenedSignal()
  {
    return mOpenedSignal;
  }

  Dali::Ui::View CreateView()
  {
    mRoot = Dali::Ui::View::New()
      .SetRequestedWidth(480.0f)
      .SetRequestedHeight(320.0f);

    return mRoot;
  }

  void Open()
  {
    mOpenedSignal.Emit(mRoot);
  }

private:
  Dali::Ui::View   mRoot;
  OpenedSignalType mOpenedSignal;
};

class AppController : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  void ConnectPane(DetailsPane& pane)
  {
    pane.OpenedSignal().Connect(this, &AppController::OnPaneOpened);
  }

private:
  void OnPaneOpened(Dali::Ui::View paneView)
  {
    mActivePane = paneView;
  }

  Dali::Ui::View mActivePane;
};
```

`Dali::BaseSignal` provides the shared implementation behind typed `Dali::Signal` objects. App code normally works with typed `Dali::Signal` accessors, while `Dali::BaseSignal::Emit()` and `Dali::BaseSignal::EmitReturn()` are called by the typed signal implementation.

## Use Return-Valued Signals

A return-valued `Dali::Signal` returns the value from the last callback invoked by `Emit()`. dali-ui uses this shape for events where a handler can report a decision, such as `Dali::Ui::InteractiveTrait::LongPressedSignal()`, which returns `Dali::Signal<bool(Dali::Ui::View, Dali::Ui::InputEvent)>`.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/dali-ui-foundation.h>

class MenuController : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  void Attach(Dali::Ui::View row)
  {
    row.AsInteractive([this](Dali::Ui::InteractiveTrait& trait) {
      trait.LongPressedSignal().Connect(this, &MenuController::OnLongPressed);
    });
  }

private:
  bool OnLongPressed(Dali::Ui::View view, Dali::Ui::InputEvent event)
  {
    mMenuTarget = view;
    return true;
  }

  Dali::Ui::View mMenuTarget;
};
```

For custom signals, use `Dali::Signal<Ret(Args...)>` when the emitter needs a returned value. If more than one callback is connected, design the signal contract so the last callback result is the value the emitter uses.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/dali-ui-foundation.h>

class PermissionRequest
{
public:
  using ResponseSignalType = Dali::Signal<bool(Dali::Ui::View)>;

  ResponseSignalType& ResponseSignal()
  {
    return mResponseSignal;
  }

  bool Request(Dali::Ui::View sourceView)
  {
    return mResponseSignal.Emit(sourceView);
  }

private:
  ResponseSignalType mResponseSignal;
};

class PermissionController : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  void Bind(PermissionRequest& request)
  {
    request.ResponseSignal().Connect(this, &PermissionController::OnPermissionRequested);
  }

private:
  bool OnPermissionRequested(Dali::Ui::View sourceView)
  {
    mLastSource = sourceView;
    return true;
  }

  Dali::Ui::View mLastSource;
};
```

## Connect Functors with `Dali::FunctorDelegate`

`Dali::Signal` can connect function objects through a `Dali::ConnectionTrackerInterface` and a functor. For `void()` callbacks, `Dali::FunctorDelegate::New()` creates a delegate object that the signal connection takes ownership of.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/dali-ui-foundation.h>

class ReadyNotifier
{
public:
  using ReadySignalType = Dali::Signal<void()>;

  ReadySignalType& ReadySignal()
  {
    return mReadySignal;
  }

  void NotifyReady()
  {
    mReadySignal.Emit();
  }

private:
  ReadySignalType mReadySignal;
};

class ReadyController : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  void Bind(ReadyNotifier& notifier)
  {
    notifier.ReadySignal().Connect(
      this,
      Dali::FunctorDelegate::New([this]() {
        OnReady();
      }));
  }

private:
  void OnReady()
  {
    mReady = true;
  }

  bool mReady{false};
};
```

For callbacks with parameters, connect a lambda or function object directly and pass a valid `Dali::ConnectionTrackerInterface` as the first `Connect()` argument.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/dali-ui-foundation.h>

class HoverModel
{
public:
  using HoverSignalType = Dali::Signal<void(Dali::Ui::View, bool)>;

  HoverSignalType& HoverSignal()
  {
    return mHoverSignal;
  }

  void SetHovered(Dali::Ui::View view, bool hovered)
  {
    mHoverSignal.Emit(view, hovered);
  }

private:
  HoverSignalType mHoverSignal;
};

class HoverController : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  void Bind(HoverModel& model)
  {
    model.HoverSignal().Connect(
      this,
      [this](Dali::Ui::View view, bool hovered) {
        OnHoverChanged(view, hovered);
      });
  }

private:
  void OnHoverChanged(Dali::Ui::View view, bool hovered)
  {
    mHoveredView = hovered ? view : Dali::Ui::View();
  }

  Dali::Ui::View mHoveredView;
};
```

## Inspect Signal State

`Dali::Signal` inherits connection-state queries from `Dali::SignalMixin`. Use `Empty()` to check whether a signal has connected callbacks, and `GetConnectionCount()` to inspect the number of connected slots.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/dali-ui-foundation.h>

class ViewEventSource
{
public:
  using ChangedSignalType = Dali::Signal<void(Dali::Ui::View)>;

  ChangedSignalType& ChangedSignal()
  {
    return mChangedSignal;
  }

  bool HasListeners() const
  {
    return !mChangedSignal.Empty();
  }

  unsigned int ListenerCount() const
  {
    return mChangedSignal.GetConnectionCount();
  }

  void NotifyChanged(Dali::Ui::View view)
  {
    if(!mChangedSignal.Empty())
    {
      mChangedSignal.Emit(view);
    }
  }

private:
  ChangedSignalType mChangedSignal;
};
```

`Dali::BaseSignal::Empty()` and `Dali::BaseSignal::GetConnectionCount()` provide the same underlying state checks for the shared signal implementation.

## Signal Behavior During Emission

`Dali::BaseSignal::Emit()` invokes the callbacks connected to a typed `Dali::Signal`. The implementation guards against nested emission of the same signal with `Dali::BaseSignal::EmitGuard`; if the same signal is emitted again while already emitting, the nested emission returns without invoking callbacks.

Connections added while a signal is already emitting are not called by that same emission pass. They are available for later emissions.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/dali-ui-foundation.h>

class RefreshModel
{
public:
  using RefreshSignalType = Dali::Signal<void(Dali::Ui::View)>;

  RefreshSignalType& RefreshSignal()
  {
    return mRefreshSignal;
  }

  void Refresh(Dali::Ui::View view)
  {
    mRefreshSignal.Emit(view);
  }

private:
  RefreshSignalType mRefreshSignal;
};

class RefreshController : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  void Bind(RefreshModel& model)
  {
    mModel = &model;
    model.RefreshSignal().Connect(this, &RefreshController::OnRefresh);
  }

private:
  void OnRefresh(Dali::Ui::View view)
  {
    if(!mRefreshInProgress)
    {
      mRefreshInProgress = true;
      view.SetBackgroundColor(Dali::Color::WHITE);
      mRefreshInProgress = false;
    }
  }

  RefreshModel* mModel{nullptr};
  bool          mRefreshInProgress{false};
};
```

Use this behavior to keep callbacks simple: connect during setup, disconnect through `Dali::ConnectionTracker` ownership, and emit custom signals from clear state transitions in your dali-ui components.
